root-cause: User input is concatenated straight into the query string, so the database can't tell the developer's SQL from the attacker's input and parses all of it as code
why-dashes: The -- comments out the leftover AND password = '...' so only the always-true OR survives; without it, AND (which binds tighter than OR) still enforces the password and the stray quote breaks the syntax
the-fix: Rewrite it as a parameterized query (prepared statement) with ? placeholders and pass the input as a separate value
